decision_metadata.title: Review of a fictional access request
decision_metadata.contextual_note: Created only for the SRS Signal research-prototype demonstration; no real case or institution is represented.

dimension_findings.concise_finding: The relied-on materials are explicitly enumerated and tied to a source register.
dimension_findings.reasoning: The supplied decision lists four material categories and states where the itemized record appears, making the visible source basis reconstructable.

supporting_quotations.text: The Chamber relied on the submitted request, the response record, the dated notice, and the applicant's written observations; each item is listed in the attached source register.
supporting_quotations.reported_document_location: Section 1 — reported location, not independently verified

dimension_findings.concise_finding: The fictional rule and the criteria applied are stated specifically.
dimension_findings.reasoning: The audit object names its fictional procedural basis and identifies the three criteria used, without requiring an outside legal conclusion.

supporting_quotations.text: This decision applies only the fictional Demonstration Procedure Rule and its stated criteria of clarity, necessity, and proportional response.
supporting_quotations.reported_document_location: Section 2 — reported location, not independently verified

limitations: The named rule is wholly fictional and is assessed only as text in the supplied audit object.

dimension_findings.concise_finding: The decision connects the documented facts to a limited corrective response.
dimension_findings.reasoning: The visible chain moves from clarity and recorded delay to the scope of the corrective direction and explains why unrelated arrangements remain untouched.

supporting_quotations.text: The request was sufficiently clear, the response record showed a documented delay, and a narrower corrective direction addressed that delay without changing unrelated arrangements.
supporting_quotations.reported_document_location: Section 3 — reported location, not independently verified

dimension_findings.concise_finding: The principal alternative proposal is recorded and answered.
dimension_findings.reasoning: The decision identifies the requested replacement direction and gives a source-linked reason for choosing the narrower response.

supporting_quotations.text: The applicant argued that a complete replacement direction was necessary. The Chamber rejects that proposal because the documented delay concerns only one response step and the source register records no defect in the remaining steps.
supporting_quotations.reported_document_location: Section 4 — reported location, not independently verified

limitations: Only the counterargument visible in the supplied fictional decision can be audited.

dimension_findings.concise_finding: The immediate effects and their relationship to the identified delay are stated.
dimension_findings.reasoning: The direction's timing and limited scope are visible, and the text connects those effects to the documented problem.

supporting_quotations.text: The direction requires a new response within ten fictional working days and leaves every other fictional arrangement unchanged; these limited effects match the identified delay.
supporting_quotations.reported_document_location: Section 5 — reported location, not independently verified

limitations: Longer-term effects are not discussed in the supplied fictional text.

dimension_findings.concise_finding: A timed reconsideration route and reasoned correction response are explicit.
dimension_findings.reasoning: The supplied text identifies how and when reconsideration may be requested and what response the Chamber must provide.

supporting_quotations.text: A written reconsideration request may be submitted to the Chamber within twenty fictional working days, and the Chamber must issue a reasoned response identifying any correction made.
supporting_quotations.reported_document_location: Section 6 — reported location, not independently verified

dimension_findings.concise_finding: The supplied decision is independently assessed as highly reconstructable overall.
dimension_findings.reasoning: This independent seventh-dimension assessment finds the source register, basis, reasoning, response, effects, and correction route coherently visible in the audit object; it is not an average of the other scores.

supporting_quotations.text: The source register, stated rule, reasoning steps, counterargument response, effects, and correction route are all recorded in this document.
supporting_quotations.reported_document_location: Section 7 — reported location, not independently verified

limitations: The result concerns only this wholly fictional supplied text and does not establish legal validity.

overall_limitations: This audit assesses only a wholly fictional demonstration text.
It does not determine legal correctness, validity, or democratic character.
